item.title: Cloud VM benchmarks 2026: performance
item.description: Published: Feb 27, 2026 on dev.to by Dimitrios Kechagias

item.title: GitHub - 0x0mer/CasNum
item.description: Contribute to 0x0mer/CasNum development by creating an account on GitHub.

item.title: A Decade of Docker Containers – Communications of the ACM
item.description: Docker is a widely used developer tool that first simplifies the assembly of an application stack (docker build), then allows for the rapid distribution of the resulting executables and data (docker push), and subsequently supports running multiple applications isolated from one another on the same machine (docker run). Developers can compile their own Docker images via a single Dockerfile alongside their source code, and reuse other published images to share packaging efforts across the global set of programming languages and application stacks.

item.title: Put the ZIP code first.
item.description: It's 2026. A ZIP code is 5 characters. From those 5 characters you get city, state, and country. 3 fields. Autofilled. Put it first, you animals.

item.title: Will Claude Code ruin our team?
item.description: AI coding is blurring the lines between engineers, PMs, and designers. In the short-term, it's going to cause havoc. What happens long-term?

item.title: Lost in Technopolis
item.description: RSS feed for John Wiegley's blog

item.title: LLMs - Solvers vs Judges
item.description: Do LLMs solve unsolvable puzzles or flag the contradiction? Testing ChatGPT, Gemini, KIMI, and others with an impossible logic puzzle reveals whether models prioritize helpfulness over truth.

item.title: Why All-in-One Productivity Apps Keep Failing | Journal it!
item.description: All-in-one productivity apps promise everything but keep disappointing. Five design traps explain why — and what solving personal productivity actually requires.

item.title: My Homemade Robot Nag
item.description: I spent way too much of my life avoiding having to make decisions and priorities. My wife and I have a goal to “do one thing on the house every day”. This usually means fixing baseboard, painting, adjusting cabinet doors, installing new cabinets. It ranges from small to big tasks and looking at the spreadsheet is very overwhelming. So work tends to happen in spurts. At the same time, cleaning and meal planning are two areas where I need to invest the mental effort to keep track of it and plan it out, but just haven’t made a focused effort.

item.title: Windows Support for FrankenPHP: It’s Finally Alive! - Kévin Dunglas
item.description: It’s happening! I am thrilled to announce the immediate availability of official Windows support for FrankenPHP. Since the project's initial release, this has been by far the most requested feature. While many of you have been successfully running FrankenPHP on Windows via WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux), native support was the "white whale" we’ve been

item.title: I made a programming language with M&Ms
item.description: I built a tiny programming language where programs are candy grids, complete with a renderer, photo decoder, AST tree, and execution trace.

item.title: Why I stopped trusting the official Wikipedia dataset, and what I did about it | Omar Kamali
item.description: It all started with a DM from a friend, member and contributor to the Moroccan Wikipedia community. "Are you using the current version of Wikipedia? The official dataset is severely outdated. We added so many cool articles nowhere on huggingface" He was right. I was running a 2023 snapshot in 2025.

item.title: Teaching Claude Code to run commands in Neovim
item.description: Inside a Neovim terminal, the $NVIM environment variable points to the parent Neovim’s Unix socket. This means you can query editor state, inspect buffers, check LSP diagnostics, and even send commands — all through Neovim’s msgpack-RPC API. What’s quite neat here is that since I run Claude Code inside a Neovim terminal window, I can make Claude use this API to query and hook into the running Neovim session and much more easily debug issues with my Neovim config, develop plugins, or help with something inside Neovim.

item.title: Why Attention Should Cost a Penny
item.description: To save our sanity from the tidalwave of spam, your inbox doesn't need a better AI - it needs a coin slot.

item.title: A Farewell to Code Reviews | Alex Martsinovich
item.description: Code reviews as I remember them are gone

item.title: Why working harder stops working
item.description: There's something nobody tells you about getting better at things.

item.title: Security vulnerabilities I found in high school
item.description: A collection of hacking stories from my high-school days in Tunisia. Some involve computers, most don't.

item.title: Why developers using AI are working longer hours
item.description: Studies find AI helps developers release more software—while logging longer hours and fixing problems after the code goes live

item.title: Ki Editor | Ki Editor
item.description: Multi-cursor structural editor

item.title: Uploading Pirated Books via BitTorrent Qualifies as Fair Use, Meta Argues * TorrentFreak
item.description: In an ongoing lawsuit, Meta now argues that uploading pirated books to strangers via BitTorrent qualifies as fair use.
